html:
[1608]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 手足 - Metaphorically refers to close family relationships, like brothers.
  • 衰麻 - Refers to mourning attire, specifically the wearing of hemp clothing and a mourning cap.
  • 启圹 - Refers to opening a grave.
  • 安厝 - Refers to the act of burying or interring.
  • 迁居 - Refers to moving to a new residence.
  • 音迹遂疏 - Indicates a decrease in communication and contact.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 寇中 - Refers to being among enemies or in a hostile environment.
  • 威猛 - Describes a severe and fierce character.
  • 友于 - A respectful term for an elder brother, often used in classical texts.
  • 讼宰 - Refers to suing the local magistrate.

Proper Nouns

  • 成 - Cheng, a character in the story.
  • 孝 - Xiao, one of Cheng's brothers.
  • 忠 - Zhong, one of Cheng's brothers.
  • 信 - Xin, one of Cheng's brothers.
  • 仁 - Ren, one of Cheng's brothers.
  • 义 - Yi, one of Cheng's brothers.
  • 友于 - Yu Yu, Cheng's elder brother.
  • 宰 - The magistrate, a local official.
  • 三泊 - A place where Yu Yu moved to.

[1609]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 效父旧行 - Follow the father's old practices.
  • 弦诵怡怡 - Harmonious and pleasant atmosphere.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 嫡出 - Legitimate offspring.
  • 庶出 - Illegitimate or concubine-born offspring.
  • 夙隙 - Long-standing grudge or conflict.
  • 圣贤 - Wise and virtuous.

Proper Nouns

  • 孝 - Name of the father.
  • 岳 - Name of the uncle.
  • 三泊 - Place name.
  • 南郡庠 - Name of the school in the southern part of the prefecture.

[1610]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 友于甚爱之 - Ambiguous phrase; '友于' can mean 'friendly' or 'affectionate,' and '甚爱之' means 'very much loved.' Could be interpreted as 'He was very much loved by his friend' or 'He was very affectionate.'
  • 祖居三泊 - Unclear phrase; '祖居' means 'ancestral home,' and '三泊' could mean 'three stops' or 'three places.' Likely refers to three different locations where the family lived.
  • 家中兄弟益不相能 - '益' means 'increasingly,' and '不相能' means 'unable to get along.' The phrase means 'The brothers in the family increasingly could not get along.'
  • 微反唇 - '微' means 'slightly' or 'a little,' and '反唇' means 'to argue back.' The phrase means 'they slightly argued back.'
  • 业诟辱庶母 - '业' is the name of a person, '诟辱' means 'to insult,' and '庶母' means 'stepmother.' The phrase means 'Yè insulted the stepmother.'
  • 功怒，刺杀业 - '功' is the name of a person, '怒' means 'angry,' and '刺杀' means 'stabbed and killed.' The phrase means 'Gōng became angry and stabbed and killed Yè.'
  • 官收功，重械之 - '官' means 'officials,' '收' means 'arrested,' '功' is the name of a person, and '重械' means 'severely punished.' The phrase means 'The officials arrested Gōng and severely punished him.'
  • 数日死狱中 - '数日' means 'several days,' and '死狱中' means 'died in prison.' The phrase means 'he died in prison after several days.'
  • 业妻冯氏，犹日以骂代哭 - '业妻' means 'Yè's wife,' '冯氏' is her surname, '犹' means 'still,' '日以' means 'every day,' and '骂代哭' means 'cursed instead of crying.' The phrase means 'Yè's wife, Feng, still cursed every day instead of crying.'
  • 功妻刘闻之，怒曰 - '功妻' means 'Gōng's wife,' '刘' is her surname, '闻之' means 'heard of it,' and '怒曰' means 'became angry and said.' The phrase means 'Gōng's wife, Liu, heard of it and became angry, saying...'
  • 汝家男子死，谁家男子活耶 - '汝家' means 'your family,' '男子' means 'men,' '死' means 'died,' '谁家' means 'whose family,' and '活耶' means 'is alive?' The phrase means 'If all the men in your family have died, whose men are still alive?'
  • 操刀入，击杀冯，自投井死 - '操刀' means 'took a knife,' '入' means 'into,' '击杀' means 'stabbed and killed,' '冯' is the name of a person, and '自投井死' means 'threw herself into a well and died.' The phrase means 'She took a knife and stabbed and killed Feng, then threw herself into a well and died.'
  • 冯父大立，悼女死惨 - '冯父' means 'Feng's father,' '大立' is his name, '悼' means 'mourned,' and '女死惨' means 'the death of his daughter was tragic.' The phrase means 'Feng's father, Dàlì, mourned the tragic death of his daughter.'
  • 率诸子弟，藏兵衣底，往捉孝妾 - '率' means 'led,' '诸子弟' means 'his sons and grandsons,' '藏兵衣底' means 'hid weapons under their clothes,' and '往捉孝妾' means 'went to capture the concubine.' The phrase means 'He led his sons and grandsons, hid weapons under their clothes, and went to capture the concubine.'
  • 裸挞道上以辱之 - '裸挞' means 'stripped and beat,' '道上' means 'on the road,' and '以辱之' means 'to humiliate her.' The phrase means 'They stripped and beat her on the road to humiliate her.'
  • 成怒曰 - '成' is the name of a person, and '怒曰' means 'became angry and said.' The phrase means 'Chéng became angry and said...'
  • 我家死人如麻，冯氏何得复尔 - '我家' means 'my family,' '死人如麻' means 'has died like straw,' '冯氏' means 'Feng's family,' and '何得复尔' means 'how can they do this again?' The phrase means 'My family has died like straw, how can Feng's family do this again?'
  • 吼奔而出 - '吼' means 'roared,' '奔' means 'rushed,' and '出' means 'out.' The phrase means 'He roared and rushed out.'
  • 诸曾从之，诸冯尽靡 - '诸曾' means 'the Zengs,' '从之' means 'followed him,' '诸冯' means 'the Fēngs,' and '尽靡' means 'were all defeated.' The phrase means 'The Zengs followed him, and the Fēngs were all defeated.'
  • 成首捉大立，割其两耳 - '成' is the name of a person, '首捉' means 'first captured,' '大立' is the name of a person, '割' means 'cut off,' and '其两耳' means 'his two ears.' The phrase means 'Chéng first captured Dàlì and cut off his two ears.'
  • 其子护救，继、绩以铁杖横击，折其两股 - '其子' means 'his son,' '护救' means 'tried to save,' '继、绩' are names of people, '以铁杖' means 'with iron staffs,' '横击' means 'struck sideways,' and '折其两股' means 'broke his two legs.' The phrase means 'His son tried to save him, but Jì and Jì struck him sideways with iron staffs and broke his two legs.'
  • 诸冯各被夷伤，哄然尽散 - '诸冯' means 'the Fēngs,' '各被夷伤' means 'were all severely injured,' and '哄然尽散' means 'scattered in a panic.' The phrase means 'The Fēngs were all severely injured and scattered in a panic.'
  • 惟冯子犹卧道周 - '惟' means 'only,' '冯子' means 'Feng's son,' '犹' means 'still,' '卧' means 'lying,' and '道周' means 'on the road.' The phrase means 'Only Feng's son was still lying on the road.'
  • 成夹之以肘，置诸冯村而还 - '成' is the name of a person, '夹之以肘' means 'held him with his elbow,' '置' means 'placed,' '诸冯村' means 'the village of the Fēngs,' and '而还' means 'and returned.' The phrase means 'Chéng held him with his elbow, placed him in the village of the Fēngs, and returned.'
  • 遂呼绩诣官自首 - '遂' means 'then,' '呼' means 'called,' '绩' is the name of a person, '诣官' means 'to the officials,' and '自首' means 'to confess.' The phrase means 'Then he called Jì to the officials to confess.'
  • 冯状亦至 - '冯状' means 'the report from Feng,' and '亦至' means 'also arrived.' The phrase means 'The report from Feng also arrived.'
  • 于是诸曾被收 - '于是' means 'thus,' '诸曾' means 'the Zengs,' and '被收' means 'were arrested.' The phrase means 'Thus, the Zengs were arrested.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 祖居三泊 - Unclear phrase; likely refers to three different locations where the family lived.
  • 家中兄弟益不相能 - Reflects the common theme in Chinese literature of family discord and the breakdown of familial harmony.
  • 业诟辱庶母 - Highlights the tension between stepchildren and stepmothers, a common theme in Chinese family dynamics.
  • 功怒，刺杀业 - Reflects the intense emotions and violence that can arise in family disputes.
  • 官收功，重械之 - Shows the role of the state in dealing with family disputes and the severity of punishment.
  • 数日死狱中 - Reflects the harshness of the legal system in ancient China.

[1611]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 惟忠亡去 - Formal opening, 'Only Zhong left,' setting the scene and introducing the main character.
  • 徘徊门外 - Means 'lingered outside the door,' indicating hesitation or uncertainty.
  • 适友于率一子一侄乡试归 - 'Just as Youyu led his son and nephew back from the provincial exams,' a formal description of their return.
  • 惊曰 - 'Surprised and said,' a common expression for a sudden reaction.
  • 弟何来 - 'Why are you here?' a direct and informal question.
  • 未语先泪 - 'Before speaking, tears came first,' a vivid description of emotional distress.
  • 长跪道左 - 'Kneeled long at the roadside,' a sign of deep respect or submission.
  • 握手拽入 - 'Grasped his hand and pulled him in,' a gesture of warmth and welcome.
  • 诘得其情 - 'Questioned and understood the situation,' a formal way of saying he got the story.
  • 逆知奇祸久矣 - 'Had long foreseen this strange calamity,' indicating premonition or prior knowledge.
  • 窜迹至此 - 'Fled to this place,' suggesting escape or hiding.
  • 匐伏而往 - 'Crawl forward,' a humble and desperate action.
  • 徒取辱耳 - 'Only bring shame,' indicating the futility of the action.
  • 但得冯父子伤重不死 - 'But if Feng's father and son survive their injuries,' a conditional statement.
  • 吾三人中幸有捷者 - 'If one of us three passes the exam,' indicating hope for success.
  • 则此祸或可少解 - 'Then this calamity might be somewhat alleviated,' a conditional outcome.
  • 留之 - 'Kept him,' indicating hospitality.
  • 昼与同餐，夜与共寝 - 'Shared meals during the day and slept together at night,' a sign of close companionship.
  • 颇感愧 - 'Felt somewhat ashamed,' indicating a sense of guilt or regret.
  • 凄然下泪 - 'Tearfully wept,' a vivid description of sadness.
  • 今始知从前非人也 - 'Now I realize that I was not a person before,' a profound realization of past wrongs.
  • 喜其悔悟 - 'Was pleased by his repentance,' indicating approval of a change in behavior.
  • 相对酸恻 - 'Looked at each other with a mix of sadness and sympathy,' a complex emotional reaction.
  • 俄报友于父子同科 - 'Soon after, it was reported that Youyu's father and son passed the same exam,' a formal announcement.
  • 祖亦副榜 - 'His grandfather also made the supplementary list,' indicating additional success.
  • 大喜 - 'Greatly delighted,' a strong emotional reaction.
  • 不赴鹿鸣 - 'Did not attend the celebratory banquet,' indicating a decision to skip the festivities.
  • 先归展墓 - 'Returned home first to pay respects at the graves,' a formal act of filial piety.
  • 明季科甲最重 - 'In the late Ming period, the imperial examinations were highly valued,' a cultural reference.
  • 诸冯皆为敛息 - 'All the Fengs became quiet and respectful,' indicating a change in behavior.
  • 托亲友赂以金粟 - 'Bribed with gold and grain through friends and relatives,' a formal description of a bribe.
  • 资其医药 - 'Provided for their medical expenses,' a formal act of assistance.
  • 讼乃息 - 'The lawsuit was settled,' indicating the resolution of a conflict.
  • 举家泣感友于 - 'The whole family wept and thanked Youyu,' a formal expression of gratitude.
  • 求其复归 - 'Asked him to return,' indicating a request for reconciliation.
  • 焚香约誓 - 'Burned incense and made an oath,' a formal act of commitment.
  • 俾各涤虑自新 - 'Made them cleanse their thoughts and reform themselves,' indicating a call for personal change.
  • 遂移家还 - 'Moved the family back,' indicating a return to their original home.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乡试 - The provincial examination, a key part of the imperial examination system.
  • 鹿鸣 - The celebratory banquet held after passing the imperial examinations, a significant social event.
  • 科甲 - Refers to the imperial examination system, particularly the highest levels of the exams.
  • 副榜 - The supplementary list of successful candidates, indicating a secondary level of achievement.
  • 明季 - The late Ming dynasty, a period of political and social turmoil.
  • 诸冯 - Refers to the Feng family, a specific clan or group of people.
  • 焚香约誓 - A traditional ritual of burning incense and making an oath, often used in formal agreements or commitments.

Proper Nouns

  • 忠 - The protagonist, Zhong.
  • 友于 - Youyu, the brother.
  • 冯父子 - Feng's father and son.
  • 鹿鸣 - The celebratory banquet.
  • 科甲 - The imperial examination system.
  • 副榜 - The supplementary list of successful candidates.
  • 明季 - The late Ming dynasty.
  • 诸冯 - The Feng family.

[1612]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 祖从叔不愿归其家 - A literal phrase indicating that the uncle (from the same clan) did not wish to return to his own family.
  • 孝乃谓友于曰 - Xiao (孝) says to Youyu (友于), where Xiao likely refers to a person's name or virtue, and Youyu could be another name or a term for a close relationship.
  • 我不德，不应有亢宗之子 - A self-deprecating statement meaning 'I am not virtuous enough to have a son who can uphold the family name.'
  • 弟又善教，俾姑为汝子 - '弟' (younger brother) is skilled in education, and '俾姑为汝子' means 'he has taught her to be your daughter-in-law.'
  • 有寸进时，可赐还也 - When there is any progress, she can be returned.
  • 又三年，祖果举于乡 - After three more years, the ancestor indeed passed the local examination.
  • 使移家，夫妻皆痛哭而去 - When they moved back to their home, both husband and wife cried bitterly and left.
  • 不数日，祖有子方三岁，亡归友于家 - Not long after, the ancestor had a son who was only three years old, and he disappeared back to Youyu's house.
  • 藏伯继善室，不肯返 - He hid in the room of the elder brother (伯继善), unwilling to return.
  • 捉去辄逃 - Whenever he was caught, he would escape.
  • 孝乃令祖异居，与友于邻 - Xiao then arranged for the ancestor to live separately but next door to Youyu.
  • 祖开户通叔家 - The ancestor opened a door connecting his house with his uncle's.
  • 两间定省如一焉 - They visited each other regularly as if they were one family.
  • 时成渐老，家事皆取决于友于 - As time passed, the elder brother grew older, and all household matters depended on Youyu.
  • 从此门庭雍穆，称孝友焉 - From then on, the family was harmonious and respected for their filial piety and brotherly love.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孝友 - Refers to filial piety and brotherly love, which are highly valued virtues in Confucian tradition.
  • 异史氏曰 - A common phrase used at the end of stories in classical literature, often followed by a commentary or moral lesson.
  • 嘉平公子 - Likely a title or nickname, possibly referring to the author or a character in the story.

Proper Nouns

  • 孝 - Likely a name or a term for filial piety.
  • 友于 - Likely a name or a term for a close relationship.
  • 祖 - The ancestor or an older family member.
  • 伯继善 - The elder brother, possibly named or known for his virtue.
  • 异史氏 - A common phrase used at the end of stories in classical literature, often followed by a commentary or moral lesson.
  • 嘉平公子 - Likely a title or nickname, possibly referring to the author or a character in the story.

[1613]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 風儀秀美 - Describes someone with elegant and beautiful appearance.
  • 區區之意，願奉終身 - A humble way of expressing a sincere wish to dedicate oneself for life.
  • 使妾清興消矣 - Making the woman's poetic inspiration disappear, indicating disappointment.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 童子試 - A traditional Chinese examination for young scholars, part of the imperial examination system.
  • 娼之門 - Refers to a brothel or a place where courtesans lived, indicating a lower social status.
  • 風流 - Charm and grace, often associated with nobility and refinement.
  • 五文新錦 - A new brocade fabric with five patterns, a valuable and luxurious item.
  • 區區之意，願奉終身 - A humble and sincere expression of dedication, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 凄風冷雨滿江城 - A poetic line describing the weather, often used to set a melancholic mood.
  • 公子如此一人，何乃不知風雅 - Criticizing the young man for not appreciating poetry and elegance.

Proper Nouns

  • 嘉平 - A family name, possibly fictional or less prominent.
  • 溫姬 - A name, possibly fictional or less prominent.
  • 宋氏 - The young man's brother-in-law, also from a prominent family.

[1614]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 蒙羞自荐 - A traditional term for a woman offering herself in marriage despite the social stigma.
  • 以貌取人 - A proverb meaning to judge by appearance.
  • 为娼 - Refers to becoming a prostitute, a serious social stigma.
  • 折帖 - Refers to folding a note or letter.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 公子 - Refers to a young nobleman or scholar.
  • 宋 - A name, likely a commoner or servant.
  • 许媪 - A woman, possibly a matchmaker or a family member.
  • 归宁 - Refers to a woman returning to her parents' home after marriage.
  • 世家文人 - Refers to a noble and cultured family.
  • 闻者传为笑谈 - Refers to the public's reaction, often spreading gossip or jokes.

Proper Nouns

  • 宋 - A name, likely a commoner or servant.
  • 许媪 - A woman, possibly a matchmaker or a family member.
  • 世家文人 - Refers to a noble and cultured family.
  • 归宁 - A term referring to a woman returning to her parents' home after marriage.
  • 蒙羞自荐 - A traditional term for a woman offering herself in marriage despite the social stigma.
  • 以貌取人 - A proverb meaning to judge by appearance.
  • 为娼 - Refers to becoming a prostitute, a serious social stigma.
  • 折帖 - Refers to folding a note or letter.
  • 闻者传为笑谈 - Refers to the public's reaction, often spreading gossip or jokes.

[1615]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 异史氏曰 - Conventional phrase used by the author of *Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio* to introduce his own commentary or moral reflection.
  • 可儿 - Affectionate term meaning 'lovely' or 'charming.'
  • 翩翩公子 - Phrase describing a handsome and elegant young man.
  • 何乃苛其中之所有哉 - Rhetorical question meaning 'how could he be so strict about what she possessed?'
  • 悔不如娼 - Hyperbolic expression meaning 'regret not being a prostitute,' indicating extreme dissatisfaction or humiliation.
  • 花菽生江 - Metaphorical expression that is unclear without further context, possibly referring to something rare or extraordinary.
  • 讹磘淫 - Phrase that is unclear without further context, possibly a misspelling or corruption; it could mean 'to mock or ridicule licentiousness.'
  • 有要宣淫、定淫者 - Phrase that is difficult to interpret clearly, possibly referring to someone who engages in or promotes licentious behavior.
  • 大小皆有 - Means 'of all sizes' or 'various kinds.'
  • 入内看物论价 - Means 'enter to look at the items and discuss the price.'
  • 崔卢之子孙如此甚众 - Refers to the descendants of the prominent families of Cui and Lu, known for their high social status.
  • 施‘恭’结缘 - Phrase that is unclear without further context, possibly referring to a religious or charitable act.
  • 亦可一笑 - Means 'it can be laughed at' or 'it's amusing.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 异史氏 - The author of *Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio*, Pu Songling.
  • 温姬 - A character in the story, likely a woman of some social standing.
  • 崔卢 - Refers to the prominent families of Cui and Lu, which were among the most influential and respected clans in ancient China.
  • 花菽生江 - Metaphorical expression that is unclear without further context, possibly referring to something rare or extraordinary.
  • 施‘恭’结缘 - Phrase that is unclear without further context, possibly referring to a religious or charitable act.

Proper Nouns

  • 异史氏 - The author of *Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio*, Pu Songling.
  • 温姬 - A character in the story.
  • 崔卢 - Refers to the prominent families of Cui and Lu.
  • 杜甫 - A famous Tang dynasty poet.
  • 《耳录》 - Another text mentioned in the commentary.
  • 花菽生江 - A metaphorical expression.
  • 子章髑髅 - A reference to Du Fu's poem.

[1616]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遇寇亂，窜入深山 - Encountered banditry or chaos; fled into the deep mountains.
  • 日既暮，村舍尚远 - As the day was drawing to a close; the village was still far away.
  • 惧遭虎狼 - Fearing to encounter tigers and wolves.
  • 遥见前途有两人，疾趁之 - Saw from afar; two people ahead; hurriedly followed them.
  • 既至，两人问客何来 - When they arrived; the two people asked the guest where he came from.
  • 殷乃自陈族贯 - Yin then introduced himself and his family lineage.
  • 两人拱敬曰 - The two people bowed respectfully and said.
  • 是良医殷先生也，仰山斗久矣 - This is the excellent physician Mr. Yin; We have long revered him like a star in the sky.
  • 殷转诘之 - Yin then questioned them.
  • 二人自言班姓，一为班爪，一为班牙 - The two people introduced themselves as the surname Ban; one was Ban Zha and the other was Ban Ya.
  • 便谓：“先生，予亦避难石室，幸可栖宿，敢屈玉趾，且有所求” - They then said: Mr., I also took refuge in a cave; it is fortunate that you can stay overnight; we dare to ask you to grace us with your presence; and we have something to ask of you.
  • 殷喜从之 - Yin happily followed them.
  • 俄至一处，室傍岩谷 - Soon they arrived at a place; a cave beside a rock valley.
  • 爇柴代烛 - Lit wood to replace candles.
  • 始见二班容躯威猛，似非良善 - When they first saw; the two Bans had fierce and imposing appearances; they did not seem good people.
  • 计无所之，亦即听之 - Having no other options; they also listened to them.
  • 又闻榻上呻吟，细审，则一老妪僵卧，似有所苦 - They also heard; moaning on the bed; upon careful examination; they found an old woman lying stiffly; seemed to be suffering from something.
  • 问：“何恙？” - Asked; what is wrong?
  • 牙曰：“以此故，敬求先生” - Ban Ya said; for this reason; we respectfully ask Mr. Yin.
  • 乃束火照榻，请客逼视 - Then lit a fire to illuminate the bed; asked the guest to examine closely.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 殷元礼，云南人，善针灸之术 - Yin Yuanli, from Yunnan, skilled in acupuncture.
  • 遇寇乱，窜入深山 - Encountered bandits or chaos; fled into the deep mountains.
  • 日既暮，村舍尚远 - As the day was drawing to a close; the village was still far away.
  • 惧遭虎狼 - Fearing to encounter tigers and wolves.
  • 遥见前途有两人，疾趁之 - Saw from afar; two people ahead; hurriedly followed them.
  • 两人问客何来，殷乃自陈族贯 - The two people asked the guest where he came from; Yin then introduced himself and his family lineage.
  • 两人拱敬曰：“是良医殷先生也，仰山斗久矣” - The two people bowed respectfully and said; This is the excellent physician Mr. Yin; We have long revered him like a star in the sky.
  • 便谓：“先生，予亦避难石室，幸可栖宿，敢屈玉趾，且有所求” - They then said: Mr., I also took refuge in a cave; it is fortunate that you can stay overnight; we dare to ask you to grace us with your presence; and we have something to ask of you.
  • 计无所之，亦即听之 - Having no other options; they also listened to them.

  • 千餘里 - A measure of distance in ancient China, where one *li* is approximately 500 meters. 'Over a thousand li' is a very long distance, suggesting supernatural or magical travel.

  • 鞚 - A term for reins or bridle, used in classical texts to describe riding.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 馬一日行五百里 - Hyperbolic expression indicating the horse travels an extraordinary distance in one day, emphasizing speed or supernatural ability.
  • 抵家，述所自来 - Classical way of expressing 'to recount one’s origin or journey.'
  • 人不之信 - Classical passive construction meaning 'people did not believe him.' The subject is omitted for stylistic effect.
  • 囊中出蜀物 - 'He took out Sichuanese items from his bag.' The phrase '囊中' refers to a bag or pouch, and '蜀物' refers to items from Shu (Sichuan), a region in ancient China.
  • 始共怪之 - 'Only then did they all find him strange/remarkable.' The word '怪' here is used in the sense of 'find strange' or 'admire as unusual.'
  • 香草久枯，恰得七茎 - 'The fragrant grass had long been dry, but he had exactly seven stalks.' This is a literal translation, but the phrase may carry symbolic or magical significance, such as in alchemy or ritual.
  • 遵方点化 - 'He followed the formula to transform [the herbs].' '点化' is a term often used in alchemy or spiritual transformation, meaning 'to transmute' or 'to transform.'
  • 家以暴富 - 'His family became suddenly wealthy.' '暴富' emphasizes sudden, unexpected wealth, often used in contexts of alchemy or divine reward.
  • 异史氏曰 - A standard heading used in the *Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio* (聊斋志异) to introduce the author's commentary or moral reflection.
  • 观桓侯燕宾，而后信武夷幔亭非诞也 - 'Observing Lord Huan entertaining his guests, one then believes that the legend of Wuyi's Manting Pavilion is not false.' This is a reference to a myth or legend.
  • 主人肃客，遂使蒙爱者几欲折肱 - 'The host received his guests with solemnity, and thus the one who was favored almost suffered a broken arm.' '折肱' is a classical idiom meaning 'to break one's arm,' often used metaphorically to suggest severe consequences or punishment.
  • 当年之勇力可想 - 'One can imagine the courage and strength of those days.' This is a concluding reflection on past heroism.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 桓侯 - Refers to Zhuangxiang Hou (庄襄侯), a title used for Zhang Liang (张良), one of the key advisors to Emperor Liu Bang of the Han dynasty. Alternatively, it may refer to Huan Hou (桓侯), a general or legendary figure. The term '桓侯' is also used in the context of Huan Hou (a deity or spirit), which may be a local deity or a historical figure venerated in a shrine.
  • 武夷幔亭 - Refers to the Manting Pavilion (幔亭) on Wuyi Mountain (武夷山) in Fujian Province, a legendary site associated with the immortal Lu Dongbin (吕洞宾) and other Daoist figures. The phrase '非诞也' suggests that the legend is not false or baseless.
  • 优戏三日 - Refers to a ritual performance or theatrical play (优戏), often associated with Daoist or folk religious practices, performed to honor a deity or ancestor.
  • 异史氏曰 - A standard phrase used by Pu Songling (蒲松龄), the author of *Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio*, to introduce his own commentary or moral reflection. This is a hallmark of the *Liaozhai* style of storytelling.
  • 点化 - A term from Daoist alchemy or spiritual transformation, often used in contexts of transmutation, enlightenment, or divine intervention.

Proper Nouns

  • 桓侯 - A title or honorific, likely referring to a historical or legendary figure, possibly Zhang Liang or a local deity.
  • 武夷幔亭 - Wuyi Mountain’s Manting Pavilion, a legendary or historical site in Fujian Province.
  • 异史氏 - A standard term used by Pu Songling to introduce his own commentary in *Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio* (聊斋志异).

  • 娘子巨家 - Refers to a wealthy or influential family, '娘子' being a respectful term for a woman.
  • 地下亦应需人 - Refers to the idea that even in the afterlife, there is a need for people to perform certain duties, reflecting traditional Chinese beliefs about the afterlife and the importance of fulfilling one's responsibilities.

Proper Nouns

  • 给孤园 - The name of the garden where the dead are cared for.
  • 娘子 - A respectful term for a woman, often used to refer to a mistress or a high-ranking woman in a household.

[1704]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 行近一舍 - Classical expression meaning 'to travel nearly a day's journey' (one *she* was about 30 kilometers).
  • 姑坐此 - Means 'please wait here,' with a polite and slightly formal tone.
  • 妾入言之 - Means 'I will go in and speak on your behalf,' using the humble pronoun *妾* ('I,' used by a woman).
  • 饲狗之役较轻 - Refers to the relatively light task of feeding dogs.
  • 当代图之 - Means 'let me arrange it for you,' with *当代* meaning 'on your behalf.'
  • 庶几得当以报 - Means 'perhaps I can find a suitable way to repay you,' with *庶几* expressing hope and *得当* meaning 'suitable' or 'appropriate.'
  • 娘子出矣 - Means 'Madam has come out,' with *娘子* being a respectful term for a lady.
  • 可使居西堂主薄 - Means 'she can appoint you as the head of the western hall,' with *主薄* referring to a position of authority.
  • 伏阶下 - Means 'bow at the steps,' a formal gesture of respect.
  • 汝以朴诚，可敬乃事 - Means 'you are honest and sincere, so I respect your work,' with *朴诚* meaning 'simple and sincere.'
  • 如有舛错，罪责不轻也 - Means 'if there are any mistakes, the consequences will be severe,' with *舛错* meaning 'mistakes' or 'errors.'
  • 旦夕所需，幸相闻 - Means 'please let me know what you need every day,' with *旦夕* referring to daily needs.
  • 录鬼籍 - Means 'to record the names of the deceased,' with *鬼籍* referring to a register of the dead.
  • 一门仆役尽来参谒 - Means 'all the servants came to pay their respects,' with *参谒* meaning 'to pay respects' or 'visit.'
  • 馈酒送脯甚多 - Means 'they brought many gifts of wine and meat,' with *脯* referring to dried meat.
  • 引嫌 - Means 'to avoid favoritism' or 'to avoid showing partiality.'
  • 悉却之 - Means 'to reject them all,' with *悉* meaning 'all' and *却之* meaning 'to reject.'
  • 日两餐皆自内出 - Means 'you have two meals a day provided from inside,' with *内* referring to the household or the lady's residence.
  • 娘子察其廉谨 - Means 'Madam noticed your integrity and diligence,' with *廉谨* meaning 'integrity and diligence.'
  • 特赐儒巾鲜衣 - Means 'she specially gave you a scholar's cap and new clothes,' with *儒巾* referring to a scholar's cap and *鲜衣* meaning 'new clothes.'
  • 凡有赍赉，皆遣春燕 - Means 'for any gifts or rewards, she always sent them through Chunyan,' with *赍赉* referring to gifts or rewards.
  • 婢颇风格 - Means 'the maid was quite elegant,' with *颇* meaning 'quite' or 'rather.'
  • 既熟，颇以眉目送情 - Means 'after becoming familiar, she often conveyed affection with her eyes and expressions,' with *眉目送情* meaning 'to convey feelings with one's eyes and expressions.'
  • 斤斤自守 - Means 'to be very cautious and self-disciplined,' with *斤斤* emphasizing carefulness.
  • 伪作騃钝 - Means 'to pretend to be simple-minded,' with *騃钝* meaning 'simple-minded' or 'stupid.'
  • 赏给倍于常廪 - Means 'your rewards were twice the usual amount,' with *常廪* referring to the standard allowance.
  • 谨抑如故 - Means 'you remained as cautious and restrained as before,' with *谨抑* meaning 'cautious and restrained.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 娘子 - A respectful term for a lady, often used by servants or lower-ranking individuals.
  • 东海薛侯女 - Refers to the daughter of a nobleman from Donghai (East Sea), indicating her high social status.
  • 儒巾鲜衣 - Scholar's cap and new clothes, symbolizing respect and recognition of the scholar's status.
  • 录鬼籍 - Recording the names of the deceased, a common practice in ancient China to keep track of deaths and perform rituals.
  • 主薄 - A position of authority, often responsible for administrative duties in a household or institution.

Proper Nouns

  • 锦瑟 - The lady's given name.
  • 东海薛侯 - The nobleman from Donghai (East Sea) who is the lady's father.
  • 春燕 - The maid's name.

[1705]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一夜方寝 - Just as he was about to sleep or at the moment of sleeping, indicating a state of rest.
  • 闻内第喊噪 - Hearing shouts and commotion inside the mansion, describing a sudden disturbance.
  • 急起捉刀出 - Rushing out with a dagger in hand, a vivid description of immediate action.
  • 见炬火光天 - Seeing the torchlight fill the sky, emphasizing the scale and intensity of the event.
  • 入窥之，则群盗充庭 - Looking inside, he saw the courtyard filled with bandits, describing the scene of intrusion.
  • 厮仆骇窜 - The servants were terrified and scattered, indicating panic and chaos.
  • 一仆促与偕遁 - A servant urged him to flee together, suggesting urgency and fear.
  • 生不肯 - He refused, showing determination or stubbornness.
  • 涂面束腰杂盗中呼曰 - He smeared his face, tied his waist, and called out among the bandits, describing a disguise and a bold action.
  • 勿惊薛娘子 - Do not frighten Lady Xue, a command to protect a specific person.
  • 但当分括财物，勿使遗漏 - Just divide and take the valuables, do not let anything be missed, instructing the bandits to act efficiently.
  • 时诸舍群贼方搜锦瑟不得 - At that time, the bandits in the various rooms were searching for Lady Xue but could not find her, indicating a failed search.
  • 生知未为所获，潜入第后独觅之 - Knowing that she had not been captured, he secretly entered the rear of the mansion to find her, showing his determination to rescue her.
  • 遇一伏妪，始知女与春燕皆越墙矣 - Encountering a hidden old woman, he learned that Lady Xue and her maid Chunyan had already escaped over the wall, revealing the outcome of the escape.
  • 生亦过墙，见主婢伏于暗陬 - He also climbed over the wall and saw the mistress and her maid crouching in a dark corner, describing the scene of their hiding place.
  • 此处乌可自匿 - How can one hide here? A rhetorical question expressing the impracticality of their hiding spot.
  • 吾不能复行矣 - I cannot walk anymore, indicating exhaustion or injury.
  • 生弃刀负之 - He discarded his dagger and carried her, showing his commitment to rescue her.
  • 奔二三里许，汗流竟体 - Ran for two or three miles, drenched in sweat, emphasizing the physical exertion.
  • 始入深谷，释肩令坐 - Finally entering a deep valley, he put her down and had her sit, providing a moment of respite.
  • 欻一虎来 - Suddenly, a tiger appeared, describing a sudden and dangerous encounter.
  • 生大骇，欲迎当之，虎已衔女 - He was greatly frightened, wanting to face it, but the tiger had already carried off Lady Xue, showing his quick reaction and the immediate danger.
  • 生急捉虎耳，极力伸臂入虎口，以代锦瑟 - He quickly grabbed the tiger's ear, and with great effort, he extended his arm into the tiger's mouth to replace Lady Xue, a heroic and desperate action.
  • 虎怒释女，嚼生臂，脆然有声 - The tiger angrily released Lady Xue, gnawing on his arm, which made a brittle sound, describing the physical injury.
  • 臂断落地，虎亦返去 - His arm fell to the ground, and the tiger also retreated, indicating the severity of the injury and the tiger's departure.
  • 女泣曰 - Lady Xue cried, expressing her emotional response.
  • 苦汝矣！苦汝矣！ - You have suffered greatly! You have suffered greatly! Repeated for emphasis.
  • 生忙遽未知痛楚，但觉血溢如水 - He was too busy to feel pain, but he only felt blood gushing out like water, showing his focus and the severity of the injury.
  • 使婢裂衿裹断处 - He had the maid tear her sash to wrap the severed part, describing the makeshift treatment.
  • 女止之，俯觅断臂，自为续之 - Lady Xue stopped her, knelt down, and tried to reattach his arm herself, showing her resourcefulness and care.
  • 乃裹之 - Then she wrapped it, completing the treatment.
  • 东方渐白，始缓步归，登堂如墟 - As dawn approached, they slowly returned, and the house seemed like a ruin, describing the aftermath and their state.
  • 天既明，仆媪始渐集 - As it became light, the servants and maids gradually gathered, indicating the return of normalcy.
  • 女亲诣西堂，问生所苦 - Lady Xue personally went to the west hall to ask about his suffering, showing her concern.
  • 解裹，则臂骨已续 - When she unwrapped it, she found that the bone had already been reconnected, indicating the success of the treatment.
  • 又出药糁其创，始去 - She then applied medicine to the wound, and only then did she leave, completing the care.
  • 由此益重生，使一切享用悉与己等 - From this, she grew even more fond of him, making sure he enjoyed all the same comforts as she did, showing her gratitude and affection.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 薛娘子 - Refers to Lady Xue, a specific person in the story.
  • 春燕 - Refers to Chunyan, Lady Xue's maid.
  • 深谷 - A deep valley, a natural setting that provides a place of refuge.
  • 虎 - The tiger, a symbol of danger and wildness in Chinese culture.
  • 仆媪 - Servants and maids, indicating the household staff.
  • 西堂 - The west hall, a specific location within the mansion.
  • 骨已续 - Bone reconnection, a reference to traditional Chinese medicine practices.

[1706]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 臂愈 - Arm healed; straightforward description of physical recovery.
  • 女置酒内室以劳之 - The woman prepared wine in the inner room to comfort him; indicates hospitality and care.
  • 赐之坐，三让而后隅坐 - She granted him a seat, he declined three times before sitting in a corner; reflects humility and proper etiquette.
  • 女举爵如让宾客 - The woman raised the cup as if offering it to guests; suggests a formal and respectful gesture.
  • 妾身已附君体，意欲效楚王女之于臣建 - My body has already attached to yours, I wish to follow the example of the Chu princess with Minister Jian; refers to a legendary romantic relationship.
  • 但无媒，羞自荐耳 - But without a matchmaker, I am ashamed to propose myself; highlights the cultural importance of arranged marriages.
  • 某受恩重，杀身不足酬 - I have received great favor, sacrificing my life would not be enough to repay it; expresses deep gratitude and loyalty.
  • 所为非分，惧遭雷殛，不敢从命 - What I ask for is beyond my due, I fear being struck by lightning, so I dare not comply; indicates fear of divine retribution.
  • 苟怜无室，赐婢已过 - If you pity me for having no wife, granting me a servant is already too much; shows modesty and appreciation.
  • 一日女长姊瑶台至 - One day, the eldest sister of the woman, named YaoTai, arrived; introduces a new character.
  • 四十许佳人也 - A beautiful woman in her forties; describes the appearance and age of YaoTai.
  • 至夕招生入，瑶台命坐 - That evening, she invited him in, and YaoTai ordered him to sit; indicates a formal and welcoming setting.
  • 我千里来为妹主婚，今夕可配君子 - I traveled a thousand miles to arrange my sister's marriage, tonight she can be paired with a gentleman; emphasizes the purpose of YaoTai's visit.
  • 生又起辞 - He stood up to decline; shows politeness and respect.
  • 瑶台遽命酒，使两人易盏 - YaoTai quickly ordered wine, making two people exchange cups; suggests a ritual or ceremony.
  • 生固辞，瑶台夺易之 - He firmly declined, but YaoTai forced him to accept; indicates YaoTai's determination.
  • 生乃伏地谢罪，受饮之 - He then fell to the ground to apologize, accepting the drink; shows submission and gratitude.
  • 实告君：妾乃仙姬，以罪被谪 - Let me tell you the truth: I am a fairy, demoted due to a sin; reveals the woman's true identity.
  • 自愿居地下收养冤魂，以赎帝谴 - I voluntarily live underground to adopt wronged souls, to redeem the emperor's punishment; explains her current role.
  • 适遭天魔之劫，遂与君有附体之缘 - I just encountered a celestial demon's calamity, which led to our physical union; describes the unusual circumstances.
  • 远邀大姊来，固主婚嫁，亦使代摄家政，以便从君归耳 - I invited my elder sister far away, to arrange the marriage, and also to manage the household, so I can follow you home; outlines the practical arrangements.
  • 既醉，归寝，欢恋臻至 - After drinking, they returned to sleep, their affection reaching its peak; describes the intimate moment.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 楚王女之于臣建 - Refers to a legendary romantic relationship between a princess of Chu and Minister Jian, symbolizing ideal love.
  • 雷殛 - Refers to divine punishment by lightning, a common motif in Chinese folklore.
  • 仙姬 - Refers to a fairy princess, a figure from Chinese mythology.
  • 地下 - Refers to the underworld, a place where spirits reside.
  • 冤魂 - Refers to wronged souls, often associated with ghosts or spirits in Chinese culture.
  • 天魔之劫 - Refers to a celestial demon's calamity, a supernatural event.
  • 地下最乐 - Refers to the idea that the underworld can be a place of joy, contrary to common perceptions.
  • 悍妇 - Refers to a domineering or fierce wife, a common trope in classical Chinese literature.
  • 屋宇隘陋 - Refers to a narrow and shabby house, indicating poverty or lack of resources.

Proper Nouns

  • 楚王女 - The princess of Chu, a legendary figure.
  • 臣建 - Minister Jian, a historical figure.
  • 瑶台 - The name of the woman's eldest sister.
  • 地下 - The underworld, a place where spirits reside.
  • 冤魂 - Wronged souls, often associated with ghosts or spirits in Chinese culture.
  • 天魔 - A celestial demon, a supernatural figure.
  • 仙姬 - A fairy princess, a figure from Chinese mythology.

[1707]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 過數日 - Means 'after several days' or 'a few days later.'

  • 請郎歸 - Means 'please return home.'
  • 君干理家事畢 - Means 'when you have finished handling household affairs.'
  • 妾當自至 - Means 'I will come myself.'
  • 以馬授生 - Means 'gave the horse to the young man.'
  • 啟扉自出 - Means 'opened the door and came out.'
  • 壁複合矣 - Means 'the wall closed again.'

  • 村人盡駭 - Means 'the villagers were all startled.'

  • 或於溝中得生履 - Means 'someone found the young man's shoes in a ditch.'
  • 疑其已死 - Means 'they suspected he was already dead.'
  • 既而年餘無耗 - Means 'after that, for more than a year there was no news.'
  • 有陝中賈某 - Means 'there was a merchant from Shaanxi.'
  • 媒通蘭氏 - Means 'arranged a marriage with the Lan family.'
  • 遂就生第與婦合 - Means 'he then moved into the young man's house and married the woman.'
  • 半年中，修建連亘 - Means 'in half a year, he built and expanded continuously.'
  • 賈出經商 - Means 'the merchant went out to do business.'
  • 又買妾歸 - Means 'he also bought a concubine and brought her back.'
  • 自此不安其室 - Means 'from then on, he did not feel at ease in his home.'
  • 生訊得其故 - Means 'the young man inquired and learned the reason.'
  • 怒，系馬而入 - Means 'angrily, he tied up the horse and entered.'
  • 見舊媪，媪驚伏地 - Means 'he saw the old maid, who was startled and fell to the ground.'
  • 生叱罵久 - Means 'the young man scolded her for a long time.'
  • 使導詣婦所 - Means 'he ordered her to lead him to the woman's place.'
  • 尋之已遁 - Means 'he searched for her but she had already fled.'
  • 既於舍後得之，已自經死 - Means 'he eventually found her behind the house, and she had already hanged herself.'
  • 遂使人舁歸蘭氏 - Means 'he then had her carried back to the Lan family.'
  • 呼妾出，年十八九，風致亦佳 - Means 'he called out the concubine, who was eighteen or nineteen years old and had a graceful appearance.'
  • 遂與寢處 - Means 'and they then lived together.'
  • 賈托村人，求反其妾 - Means 'the merchant entrusted the villagers to ask for the return of his concubine.'
  • 妾哀號不肯去 - Means 'the concubine wept and refused to leave.'
  • 生乃具狀 - Means 'the young man then prepared a statement.'
  • 將訟其霸產占妻之罪 - Means 'to sue him for seizing property and taking a wife by force.'
  • 賈不敢復言 - Means 'the merchant dared not speak again.'
  • 收肆西去 - Means 'he gathered his things and left westward.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 冥會 - Refers to a meeting in the afterlife, a concept from Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 高廬 - Refers to a grand or high house, often used to describe a luxurious residence.
  • 箠楚 - Refers to beating as punishment, a form of corporal punishment.
  • 媒通 - Refers to arranging a marriage through a matchmaker.
  • 自經 - Refers to hanging oneself, a method of suicide.
  • 舁 - Refers to carrying on a stretcher, often used in the context of moving a body.
  • 具狀 - Refers to preparing a formal statement or complaint, often used in legal contexts.
  • 霸產 - Refers to seizing property by force.
  • 占妻 - Refers to taking a wife by force.
  • 收肆 - Refers to gathering one's belongings and leaving, often used in the context of abandoning a place.

Proper Nouns

  • 生 - Refers to the young man, the main character of the story.
  • 妾 - Refers to the young man's wife.
  • 賈某 - Refers to the merchant from Shaanxi.
  • 蘭氏 - Refers to the Lan family, the young man's wife's family.
  • 舊媪 - Refers to the old maid, a servant in the young man's household.
  • 妾 - Refers to the concubine, a woman the merchant bought and brought back.
  • 村人 - Refers to the villagers, the people living in the village where the young man returns.

  • 业多，则割爱难矣 - '业多' means 'many offspring,' and '割爱难矣' means 'it is hard to part with them.'
  • 婢不复产 - '婢不复产' means 'the maid no longer gives birth.'
  • 忽携老仆夜出，亦不返 - '忽携老仆夜出，亦不返' means 'suddenly took an old servant out at night and did not return.'

  • 锦瑟 - A metaphor for a beautiful object or person, often used in classical literature.

[1710]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 推折獄才 - Phrase meaning 'to recommend someone for their talent in handling legal cases'; '推' means 'to recommend', '折獄' means 'to handle legal cases'.
  • 寄監 - Means 'to detain in prison'; '寄' means 'to detain', '監' means 'prison'.
  • 質理聽用 - Means 'to prepare tools for examination'; '質' means 'to prepare', '理' means 'tools', '聽用' means 'for examination'.
  • 嚴刑自有桎梏 - Phrase meaning 'there are already shackles for severe punishment'; '嚴刑' means 'severe punishment', '自有' means 'already have', '桎梏' means 'shackles'.
  • 非刑折獄 - Means 'to use unusual methods to solve cases'; '非刑' means 'unusual methods', '折獄' means 'to solve cases'.
  • 姑婦 - Refers to the grandmother and daughter-in-law, a common term in classical Chinese family contexts.
  • 堂上刀石具在 - Means 'the knives and stones are all on the hall'; '堂上' means 'on the hall', '刀石' means 'knives and stones', '具在' means 'all present'.
  • 可自取击杀之 - Means 'you can take and kill him'; '可' means 'can', '自取' means 'take yourself', '击杀' means 'kill', '之' is a pronoun referring to the person to be killed.
  • 趑趄 - Means 'hesitant or reluctant', often used to describe someone's demeanor.
  • 邂逅抵偿 - Means 'accidental retribution'; '邂逅' means 'accidental', '抵偿' means 'retribution'.
  • 无虑 - Means 'do not worry'; '无' means 'not', '虑' means 'worry'.
  • 掇石交投 - Means 'to pick up stones and throw them at each other'; '掇' means 'to pick up', '石' means 'stones', '交投' means 'to throw at each other'.
  • 衔恨已久 - Means 'has been holding a grudge for a long time'; '衔恨' means 'hold a grudge', '已久' means 'for a long time'.
  • 逡巡未下 - Means 'hesitated and did not strike'; '逡巡' means 'hesitated', '未下' means 'did not strike'.
  • 执媪严梏之 - Means 'to arrest the grandmother and severely punish her'; '执' means 'to arrest', '媪' means 'grandmother', '严' means 'severely', '梏' means 'punish'.
  • 笞无赖三十 - Means 'to cane the rascal thirty times'; '笞' means 'cane', '无赖' means 'rascal', '三十' means 'thirty'.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 適世家园中有虎阱 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'In the world's home there is a tiger pit,' used to describe a dangerous situation.
  • 揉木为圈，结绳作网，以覆其口 - A descriptive phrase meaning 'shaping wood into a ring, tying ropes to make a net, covering its mouth,' describing the construction of a trap.
  • 生坠网上，网为之侧，以腹受网，身半倒悬 - A descriptive phrase meaning 'falling onto the net, the net tilting to the side, receiving the net with the belly, half hanging upside down,' describing the victim's position in the trap.
  • 下视，虎蹲阱中，仰见卧人，跃上，近不盈尺，心胆俱碎 - A descriptive phrase meaning 'looking down, the tiger crouching in the pit, looking up to see a lying person, jumping up, close not more than a foot, heart and courage shattered,' describing the intense fear experienced by the victim.
  • 园丁来饲虎，见而怪之，扶上，已死。移时渐苏，备言其故 - A descriptive phrase meaning 'the gardener came to feed the tiger, seeing it and finding it strange, helping him up, already dead. After a while gradually reviving, fully explaining the reason,' describing the rescue and recovery process.
  • 其地乃浙界，离家已四百余里矣 - A descriptive phrase meaning 'the place is in Zhejiang, already four hundred miles away from home,' indicating the distance from the victim's home.
  • 主人赠以资遣归 - A descriptive phrase meaning 'the owner gave him money to send him back home,' describing the reward given to the victim.

  • 狐 - A fox, often associated with cunning and trickery in Chinese folklore.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sequence '適世家园中有虎阱，揉木为圈，结绳作网，以覆其口' must be read as a single description of the construction of a trap.
  • The clause '生坠网上，网为之侧，以腹受网，身半倒悬' describes the victim's position in the trap.
  • The clause '下视，虎蹲阱中，仰见卧人，跃上，近不盈尺，心胆俱碎' describes the intense fear experienced by the victim.
  • The clause '园丁来饲虎，见而怪之，扶上，已死。移时渐苏，备言其故' describes the rescue and recovery process.
  • The clause '其地乃浙界，离家已四百余里矣' indicates the distance from the victim's home.
  • The clause '主人赠以资遣归' describes the reward given to the victim.
  • The clause '归告人：‘虽得两次死，然非狐则贫不能归也。’' reflects the victim's gratitude and acknowledgment of the fox's help.
